message:
chore(beads): file 3 reality-check findings (v1nwd, 4pc88, m1g5j)

- v1nwd (P1): connected_components et al return list<list> instead of
  upstream generator<set> — concrete drop-in-compat gap.
- 4pc88 (P3): README Graph Types section omits MultiGraph /
  MultiDiGraph despite parity-green status.
- m1g5j (P3): README/FEATURE_PARITY Current State counts are 10 days
  stale (e.g. '89 NX-delegated' → actual 0; '1394 tests' → 3879).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Preserve current mode for reset paths, serialize mode in pickle state, add regression tests, and validate with rch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-16T02:35:38.086728148Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-16T02:40:09.098837166Z","closed_at":"2026-04-16T02:40:09.096836107Z","close_reason":"Preserved Python wrapper mode across reset and pickle restore; validated with rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"franken_networkx-4pc88","title":"README 'Graph Types' section missing MultiGraph and MultiDiGraph","description":"``README.md`` lines 86-89::

    ## Graph Types

    - \`Graph\` -- undirected graph
    - \`DiGraph\` -- directed graph (algorithms automatically convert to undirected where needed)

But ``FEATURE_PARITY.md`` line 72 reports:

    | MultiGraph/MultiDiGraph | parity_green | Full method parity with Graph/DiGraph
    (34 methods + 6 view types). Algorithm dispatch supports all 4 graph types
    via automatic simple-graph projection. Backend conversion round-trips work. |

And the V1 Scope section at README line 156 lists::

    Graph, DiGraph, MultiGraph core semantics; ...

And the code:

    >>> import franken_networkx as fnx
    >>> for cls in ('Graph', 'DiGraph', 'MultiGraph', 'MultiDiGraph'):
    ...     print(cls, hasattr(fnx, cls))
    Graph True
    DiGraph True
    MultiGraph True
    MultiDiGraph True

The README's user-facing "Graph Types" section is incomplete — two of
the four shipped, parity-green graph classes are undocumented.
First-time readers won't know fnx supports MultiGraph / MultiDiGraph.

Fix: append two lines to README's Graph Types section::

    - \`MultiGraph\` -- undirected multigraph (parallel edges with integer keys)
    - \`MultiDiGraph\` -- directed multigraph

Also remove the "(algorithms automatically convert to undirected where needed)"
parenthetical on DiGraph — that's both unclear and describes an
internal dispatch detail, not a user-facing property.

Non-scope: no code changes, just a README touch-up.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:18:42.301187105Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:18:42.301187105Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"franken_networkx-4sed","title":"NX-REPLACE: Graph operators — line_graph, inverse_line_graph, disjoint_union, power, stochastic, etc.","description":"## Functions to replace (12)
1. line_graph (__init__.py:1458) — edges become nodes, shared endpoints become edges
2. inverse_line_graph (__init__.py:3806) — reverse of line_graph (Whitney's theorem)
3. make_max_clique_graph (__init__.py:1473) — clique graph construction
4. disjoint_union (__init__.py:1496) — union with renamed nodes
5. disjoint_union_all (__init__.py:2311) — union of multiple graphs
6. power (__init__.py:1487) — G^k: edge if distance <= k
7. stochastic_graph (__init__.py:1367) — normalize edge weights to transition probabilities
8. projected_graph (__init__.py:960) — bipartite projection
9. create_empty_copy (__init__.py:1211) — graph with same nodes but no edges
10. minimum_edge_cut (__init__.py:1360) — already have Rust impl, just needs rewiring
11. minimum_st_node_cut (__init__.py:3328) — already have Rust impl, just needs rewiring
12. ego_graph (__init__.py:1383) — already have Rust impl, Python overrides it

## Implementation plan
- Group A (already in Rust, just rewire Python): minimum_edge_cut, minimum_st_node_cut, ego_graph, create_empty_copy — these are quick wins
- Group B (simple construction): disjoint_union, disjoint_union_all, stochastic_graph, power
- Group C (algorithmic): line_graph, inverse_line_graph, projected_graph, make_max_clique_graph

## Files to modify
- python/franken_networkx/__init__.py: rewire Group A immediately
- crates/fnx-algorithms/src/lib.rs: implement Group B and C
- crates/fnx-python/src/algorithms.rs: add bindings

## Acceptance criteria
- All 12 functions match NX output on test graphs
- No _to_nx() calls remaining in these functions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:18:53.572338891Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:47:52.934939477Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:47:52.934684785Z","close_reason":"Superseded by owcd (quick-wins) and qwd5 (new impls) for better granularity","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"franken_networkx-m1g5j","title":"README 'Current State' block (stamped 2026-04-13) has 9 stale claims; either refresh or remove","description":"README.md lines 141-152 contain a "Current State (as of 2026-04-13)"
block whose claims have drifted significantly from ``main`` at this
session's HEAD. The block reads like project-marketing truth but
embeds nine factually wrong numbers:

| Claim in README line | README value | Actual at HEAD |
|----------------------|--------------|----------------|
| Line 145: public exports total         | 446   | **801**    |
| Line 145: RUST_NATIVE                  | 41    | **118**    |
| Line 145: PY_WRAPPER                   | 306   | **660**    |
| Line 145: NX-delegated                 | 89    | **0**      |
| Line 146: parity tests pass            | 1394  | **~3879**  |
| Line 146: parity tests skipped         | 54    | **84**     |
| Line 146: suite runtime                | ~20s  | ~24s       |
| Line 152: beads closed                 | 299   | **993**    |
| Line 152: beads open                   | 82    | **4**      |

In particular:

- **"89 NX-delegated" is wrong.** It's 0 now (verified against
  docs/coverage.md which is the machine-checked source of truth).
- **"Eliminate remaining NX delegations: 89 functions still delegate
  to NX; each is either exotic..."** (README line 186) — completed
  this session; no delegated exports remain.

FEATURE_PARITY.md has the same problem — lines 40-45 claim 779 exports
/ 239 RUST_NATIVE / 519 PY_WRAPPER / 19 classes / 2 constants; actual is
801 / 118 / 660 / 21 / 2.

Fix options (pick one):

**Option A (recommended): replace prose counts with doc references.**
Delete the hand-maintained numbers; refer to ``docs/coverage.md`` for
the machine-checked export inventory. The "Current State" block then
doesn't rot each release. Tests counts similarly can point to
``pytest tests/python/`` and the suite's own summary line.

**Option B: bump every number to HEAD and accept future drift.** Less
durable but cheaper.

Non-scope:

- README's "Supported Algorithms" table (line 49-84) is structurally
  fine — all 201 cited names exist in ``fnx.__all__`` (verified via
  grep + attribute lookup). No symbol drift there.
- README's "Next Steps" bullet #4 ("Eliminate remaining NX
  delegations: 89 functions still delegate") is resolved and should
  be removed as part of the fix.

Audit note: filed from /reality-check-for-project pass. Low priority
(P3) because it's doc-debt, not a correctness issue.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:19:14.791304788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:19:14.791304788Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-m1r","title":"I/O Format Expansion: GEXF, Pajek, LEDA, sparse6/graph6, adjlist variants","description":"## Scope
Implement ~26 I/O functions across multiple graph file formats:

### GEXF Format
- `read_gexf` / `write_gexf` — Graph Exchange XML Format (used by Gephi)
- `generate_gexf` — generate GEXF lines
- `parse_gexf` — parse GEXF string

### Pajek Format
- `read_pajek` / `write_pajek` — Pajek .net format
- `parse_pajek` — parse Pajek string
- `generate_pajek` — generate Pajek lines

### LEDA Format
- `read_leda` / `write_leda` — LEDA native format
- `parse_leda` — parse LEDA string

### sparse6 / graph6
- `from_sparse6_bytes` / `to_sparse6_bytes`
- `from_graph6_bytes` / `to_graph6_bytes`
- `read_sparse6` / `write_sparse6`
- `read_graph6` / `write_graph6`

### Adjlist / Edgelist Variants
- `read_multiline_adjlist` / `write_multiline_adjlist`
- `read_weighted_edgelist` / `write_weighted_edgelist`
- `parse_adjlist` / `generate_adjlist`
- `parse_edgelist` / `generate_edgelist`

## Implementation Location
- Rust: `crates/fnx-readwrite/src/` — new format modules for performance
- Python: `python/franken_networkx/readwrite/` — bindings and format-specific wrappers

## Approach
- Can be implemented incrementally by format family
- sparse6/graph6 are compact binary encodings — Rust implementation recommended
- GEXF is XML-based — use quick-xml crate in Rust
- Pajek/LEDA are line-based text formats — straightforward parsing

## Edge Cases
- Unicode node labels
- Attribute preservation (GEXF has rich attribute support)
- Multigraph encoding/decoding
- Large file streaming (don't load entire file into memory)
- Malformed input handling with clear error messages

## Test Requirements
- Round-trip tests: write then read should produce identical graph
- Cross-validate against NetworkX for each format
- Test with real-world graph files from SNAP/KONECT datasets
- Malformed input rejection tests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-21T19:12:15.592143950Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T07:35:53.678999365Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T07:35:53.678553020Z","close_reason":"All sub-beads closed: GEXF (omw), Pajek/LEDA (l34), sparse6/graph6 (guk), parse/generate (fh8). I/O format expansion complete.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io","readwrite","tier-2"]}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-m2o1","title":"parity: graph classes should accept extra attr kwargs in add_weighted_edges_from","description":"Graph, DiGraph, MultiGraph, and MultiDiGraph currently reject extra keyword attributes in add_weighted_edges_from(...) with TypeError, while upstream NetworkX applies those attrs to every inserted edge. For example, add_weighted_edges_from([('a','b',1),('b','c',2)], color='red') succeeds in NetworkX and stores both weight and color, but FrankenNetworkX raises. Match the upstream add_weighted_edges_from(..., **attr) contract across the core graph classes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:02:31.524396088Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T19:21:37.372443431Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T19:21:19.052540027Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":90,"issue_id":"franken_networkx-m2o1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Fixed in latest commit. add_weighted_edges_from wrapped on all 4 classes — fast-path Rust call when no attr kwargs, per-edge add_edge loop when attrs are present. 10 parity tests pass.","created_at":"2026-04-22T19:21:37Z"}]}

On a simple path a-b-c-d, FrankenNetworkX yields {'d': ['c'], 'c': ['b'], 'b': ['a'], 'a': []} while NetworkX yields {'a': [], 'b': ['a'], 'c': ['b'], 'd': ['c']}. Preserve NetworkX's observable mapping order, including cutoff queries.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:08:32.277055305Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T15:35:06.805515667Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T15:35:06.803671365Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"franken_networkx-v1m8","title":"networkx: max_weight_matching maxcardinality kwarg","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:26:03.035096275Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T05:30:05.896451745Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T05:30:05.894239138Z","close_reason":"Implemented max_weight_matching maxcardinality kwarg parity","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"franken_networkx-v1nwd","title":"Component functions return list<list> instead of generator<set> — breaks drop-in compatibility","description":"README.md line 7 states: "FrankenNetworkX is a high-performance,
Rust-backed drop-in replacement for NetworkX."

The following six exported functions materially diverge from the
upstream nx return-type contract, breaking drop-in compatibility:

| Function                          | nx returns           | fnx returns        |
|-----------------------------------|----------------------|--------------------|
| ``connected_components``          | ``generator[set]``   | ``list[list]``     |
| ``biconnected_components``        | ``generator[set]``   | ``list[list]``     |
| ``strongly_connected_components`` | ``generator[set]``   | ``list[list]``     |
| ``weakly_connected_components``   | ``generator[set]``   | ``list[list]``     |
| ``articulation_points``           | ``generator[node]``  | ``list[node]``     |
| ``simple_cycles``                 | ``generator[list]``  | ``list[list]``     |

Reproducer::

    import networkx as nx, franken_networkx as fnx

    gn = nx.Graph(); gn.add_edges_from([(0,1),(1,2),(3,4)])
    gf = fnx.Graph(); gf.add_edges_from([(0,1),(1,2),(3,4)])

    cc_n = nx.connected_components(gn)
    cc_f = fnx.connected_components(gf)

    # These differ:
    assert type(cc_n).__name__ == 'generator'   # nx
    assert type(cc_f).__name__ == 'list'        # fnx (should be generator)

    # Element type also differs:
    first_n = next(iter(cc_n))
    first_f = cc_f[0]
    assert isinstance(first_n, set)             # nx
    assert isinstance(first_f, list)            # fnx (should be set)

Why this matters:

- ``next(iter(fnx.connected_components(G)))`` still works (list is
  iterable), but any code depending on streaming semantics (early
  termination, lazy evaluation on large graphs, generator-only
  protocols) breaks.
- ``set`` membership semantics differ from ``list`` — ``node in
  component`` is O(1) vs O(n); ``set & set`` works, ``list & list``
  doesn't.
- nx's own API guarantee is documented as generator-returning and
  upstream algorithms (e.g. ``nx.is_connected`` implementations) rely
  on this shape.

Scope:

- ``attracting_components``, ``bridges``, ``cycle_basis`` already
  return generators (verified).
- ``find_cliques`` and other clique-enumeration functions: audit as
  part of this bead or split off if shape differs.

Fix plan:

1. Wrap each offending public function so it yields (generator) and
   the per-component container is a ``set`` (not ``list``). Keep the
   internal Rust call returning ``Vec<Vec<NodeId>>`` but convert at
   the Python boundary.
2. Audit for callers inside ``python/franken_networkx/__init__.py``
   that depend on list-of-list shape and convert via ``list()`` at
   those sites.
3. Regression tests: add a test file
   ``tests/python/test_generator_return_parity.py`` that asserts
   ``type(...).__name__ == 'generator'`` and element type is ``set``
   for each of the six functions.

Priority: P1 — direct drop-in-replacement contract violation named in
the README.

Audit note: this gap was present before the current session; discovered
during the /reality-check-for-project review pass.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:18:23.062846330Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:18:23.062846330Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-v3y","title":"Advanced Centrality: current-flow betweenness, subset betweenness, Laplacian centrality","description":"## Scope
Implement ~20 advanced centrality functions:

### Current-Flow Betweenness Family
- `current_flow_betweenness_centrality` — based on electrical current flow
- `edge_current_flow_betweenness_centrality` — edge variant
- `approximate_current_flow_betweenness_centrality` — randomized approximation
- `current_flow_closeness_centrality` — closeness based on effective resistance

### Subset Betweenness Family
- `betweenness_centrality_subset` — betweenness restricted to source/target subsets
- `edge_betweenness_centrality_subset` — edge variant of subset betweenness

### Other Centrality Measures
- `edge_load_centrality` — load centrality for edges
- `laplacian_centrality` — based on Laplacian spectrum drop
- `percolation_centrality` — based on percolation states
- `information_centrality` — based on information flow (related to current-flow)
- `second_order_centrality` — based on random walk return times
- `subgraph_centrality` — based on subgraph counts (matrix exponential)
- `subgraph_centrality_exp` — explicit matrix exponential computation
- `communicability_betweenness_centrality` — based on communicability
- `estrada_index` — trace of matrix exponential (related to subgraph centrality)
- `trophic_levels` — trophic level in food webs
- `trophic_differences` — differences between connected trophic levels
- `trophic_incoherence_parameter` — measure of trophic incoherence
- `group_betweenness_centrality` — betweenness for a group of nodes
- `group_closeness_centrality` — closeness for a group of nodes

## Algorithmic Approach
- Current-flow variants: Solve Laplacian linear systems (L * x = b). Use scipy.sparse.linalg.spsolve or conjugate gradient. The Laplacian pseudoinverse gives effective resistances.
- Subset betweenness: Modify standard betweenness to only count paths from source set to target set.
- Laplacian centrality: Compute Laplacian eigenvalues, measure spectral impact of node removal.
- Percolation centrality: Weighted variant of betweenness using percolation states.
- Second-order centrality: Analyze variance of random walk return times.
- Subgraph centrality / communicability: Compute matrix exponential exp(A), diagonal entries give subgraph centrality.

## Dependencies
- Requires spectral/matrix functions (already implemented)
- numpy/scipy for linear algebra

## Test Requirements
- Cross-validate against NetworkX for small graphs (exact computation is expensive)
- Verify symmetry properties (undirected graphs)
- Verify normalization
- Performance benchmarks for approximate variants","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-21T19:11:48.521610696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T18:49:30.019156587Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T18:49:30.018635140Z","close_reason":"Implemented 18 advanced centrality functions: current_flow_betweenness (+ edge + approx + closeness), betweenness/edge_betweenness_subset, edge_load, laplacian, percolation, information, second_order, subgraph_exp, communicability_betweenness, trophic_levels/differences/incoherence, group_betweenness/closeness. Total: 579.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["algorithms","centrality","tier-2"]}

 {"id":"franken_networkx-v62u","title":"global non_neighbors wrapper mismatches NetworkX on directed and missing-node cases","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:41:30.025359756Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:01.933922457Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:01.932047700Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
